title: euploca pachyphylla
intro: Euploca pachyphylla, also known as the 'Mexican gold poppy', is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). Euploca pachyphylla has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating various skin conditions and wounds. Euploca pachyphylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
